paragraphs.text:
%md
# NYC Yellow Taxi 2024 — EDA Dashboard
**Course:** CSCI461 Introduction to Big Data (Spring 2026)  
**Tool:** Apache Zeppelin 0.10.1 — Visualization / Reporting  
**Data source:** HDFS — `hdfs://namenode:9000/taxi/raw/`  
**Dataset:** 35,602,215 rows × 25 columns (after preprocessing)

%pyspark
# ── 1. Initialise SparkSession and load data from HDFS ────────────────────────
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, count, avg, round, desc, month, hour

spark = SparkSession.builder \
    .appName("NYC Taxi EDA — Zeppelin") \
    .config("spark.executor.memory", "2g") \
    .getOrCreate()

HDFS_PATH = "hdfs://namenode:9000/taxi/raw"
df = spark.read.parquet(HDFS_PATH)

print(f"Rows loaded from HDFS : {df.count():,}")
print(f"Columns               : {len(df.columns)}")
print(f"Schema:")
df.printSchema()

%pyspark
# ── 2. Trips and Average Fare by Pickup Hour ─────────────────────────────────
# Switch to Bar Chart view in Zeppelin to visualise this table
from pyspark.sql.functions import hour as spark_hour

trips_by_hour = df \
    .withColumn("pickup_hour", spark_hour(col("tpep_pickup_datetime"))) \
    .groupBy("pickup_hour") \
    .agg(
        count("*").alias("trip_count"),
        round(avg("fare_amount"), 2).alias("avg_fare")
    ) \
    .orderBy("pickup_hour")

trips_by_hour.show(24, truncate=False)

%pyspark
# ── 3. Monthly Trip Volume and Average Fare ───────────────────────────────────
# Switch to Bar Chart view — keys: pickup_month, values: trip_count or avg_fare
from pyspark.sql.functions import month as spark_month

monthly_stats = df \
    .withColumn("pickup_month", spark_month(col("tpep_pickup_datetime"))) \
    .groupBy("pickup_month") \
    .agg(
        count("*").alias("trip_count"),
        round(avg("fare_amount"), 2).alias("avg_fare"),
        round(avg("trip_distance"), 2).alias("avg_distance")
    ) \
    .orderBy("pickup_month")

monthly_stats.show(12, truncate=False)

%pyspark
# ── 4. Fare Distribution by Payment Type ─────────────────────────────────────
# payment_type: 1=Credit Card, 2=Cash, 3=No Charge, 4=Dispute, 5=Unknown
# Switch to Pie Chart view to see payment method breakdown

payment_map = {1: "Credit Card", 2: "Cash", 3: "No Charge", 4: "Dispute", 5: "Unknown", 6: "Voided"}

from pyspark.sql.functions import when

payment_stats = df \
    .withColumn("payment_label",
        when(col("payment_type") == 1, "Credit Card")
        .when(col("payment_type") == 2, "Cash")
        .when(col("payment_type") == 3, "No Charge")
        .when(col("payment_type") == 4, "Dispute")
        .otherwise("Other")
    ) \
    .groupBy("payment_label") \
    .agg(
        count("*").alias("trip_count"),
        round(avg("fare_amount"), 2).alias("avg_fare")
    ) \
    .orderBy(desc("trip_count"))

payment_stats.show(truncate=False)

%pyspark
# ── 5. Top 10 Busiest Pickup Locations ───────────────────────────────────────
# Switch to Bar Chart — keys: PULocationID, values: trip_count

top_locations = df \
    .groupBy("PULocationID") \
    .agg(
        count("*").alias("trip_count"),
        round(avg("fare_amount"), 2).alias("avg_fare")
    ) \
    .orderBy(desc("trip_count")) \
    .limit(10)

top_locations.show(truncate=False)

%pyspark
# ── 6. Weekend vs Weekday — Trip Count and Average Fare ───────────────────────
# Switch to Bar Chart — keys: day_type, values: trip_count or avg_fare
from pyspark.sql.functions import dayofweek

weekend_stats = df \
    .withColumn("pickup_day", dayofweek(col("tpep_pickup_datetime"))) \
    .withColumn("day_type",
        when(col("pickup_day").isin([1, 7]), "Weekend")
        .otherwise("Weekday")
    ) \
    .groupBy("day_type") \
    .agg(
        count("*").alias("trip_count"),
        round(avg("fare_amount"), 2).alias("avg_fare"),
        round(avg("trip_distance"), 2).alias("avg_distance")
    ) \
    .orderBy("day_type")

weekend_stats.show(truncate=False)

print("\nKey insight: Weekend trips tend to have higher fares due to longer night-time rides.")

%md
## Summary

| Visualisation | Chart Type | Key Finding |
|---|---|---|
| Trips by hour | Bar chart | Peak demand at 6 PM (2,555,714 trips); lowest at 4 AM |
| Monthly stats | Bar chart | Highest fares in September ($20.72 avg) |
| Payment type | Pie chart | Credit card dominates (~68% of trips) |
| Top locations | Bar chart | Location IDs 132, 161, 237 are busiest |
| Weekend vs Weekday | Bar chart | Weekdays have more trips; weekends have higher avg fares |

**Data source:** `hdfs://namenode:9000/taxi/raw/`  
**Visualization tool:** Apache Zeppelin 0.10.1
